message.content:
Search the web for the correct npx-runnable npm package names for these MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. I need the exact package name and correct command/args format for running them via npx:

1. GitHub MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-github or similar)
2. Puppeteer MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer or similar)
3. Slack MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-slack or similar)
4. Filesystem MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem or similar)
5. Memory MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory or similar)
6. Sequential Thinking MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking or similar)
7. Brave Search MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search or similar)
8. Google Maps MCP server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps or similar)

For each one, tell me:
- The correct npm package name
- The command to run it (npx -y package-name)
- Any required environment variables

Do NOT include code to write files. Just return the research results.

content.text: I'll search for the correct npm package names and commands for these MCP servers.

## Search Results Summary

I found information about both npm packages from the Model Context Protocol ecosystem:

### @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem

The latest version is 2026.1.14 with 171,238 weekly downloads. This MCP server is licensed under the MIT License, allowing free use, modification, and distribution.

To use it with npx, you can run:
```
npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /path/to/allowed/dir
```

It's a Node.js server implementing Model Context Protocol (MCP) for filesystem operations.

### @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack

This is an MCP Server for the Slack API, enabling Claude to interact with Slack workspaces. However, the package is no longer supported, and you should contact Support at https://www.npmjs.com/support for more info.

To use it with npx:
```
npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack
```

The configuration would require environment variables like `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` and `SLACK_TEAM_ID` to function properly.

I found relevant information about these npm packages. Here's what I found:

## @modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search

You can install @modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search in your project by running `npm i @modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search`. There are 14 other projects in the npm registry using @modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search. This MCP server is licensed under the MIT License, meaning you are free to use, modify, and distribute the software, subject to the terms and conditions of the MIT License.

## @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps

You can start using @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps in your project by running `npm i @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps`. There is 1 other project in the npm registry using @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps. This MCP server is licensed under the MIT License, meaning you are free to use, modify, and distribute the software, subject to the terms and conditions of the MIT License.

## 2026 Usage

Both Google Maps and Brave Search MCP Servers are featured among the top MCP servers recommended for 2026. Brave Search provides web and local search using Brave's Search API, while Google Maps offers location services, directions, and place details.

### @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory

This package was published on 2026.1.26 (January 26, 2026). The Memory server implements a knowledge graph-based system for persistent memory, specifically designed for integration with Claude, allowing LLMs to store and retrieve information about entities and their relationships across conversation sessions.

You can install it using:
- `npm i @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory`
- Or via npx: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory`

### @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking

This package was published on 2025.12.18 (December 18, 2025). The Sequential Thinking server structures problem-solving as an explicit sequence of thoughts, with reflection steps that can guide the LLM to reconsider and refine its approach.

You can install it using:
- `npm i @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking`
- Or via npx: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking`

Both packages are licensed under the MIT License, meaning you are free to use, modify, and distribute the software, subject to the terms and conditions of the MIT License.

Based on the search results, here's information about the official MCP (Model Context Protocol) npm packages you requested:

## Official @modelcontextprotocol Packages

Official reference servers include: Everything (reference/test server with prompts, resources, and tools), Fetch (web content fetching and conversion), Filesystem (secure file operations), Git (tools to read, search, and manipulate Git repositories), Memory (knowledge graph-based persistent memory system), and Sequential Thinking (dynamic problem-solving through thought sequences).

### @modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer

This MCP server is licensed under the MIT License, allowing you to use, modify, and distribute the software subject to the terms and conditions of the MIT License. The Puppeteer MCP server is a comprehensive Model Context Protocol server that provides browser automation capabilities, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to interact with web pages, take screenshots, and execute JavaScript in both local and cloud environments.

### @modelcontextprotocol/server-github

The GitHub server is part of the official reference servers and can be configured with a GitHub personal access token for use with LLMs.

## Additional Reference Servers

The @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything package is versioned 2026.1.26 and was published 13 days ago. This MCP server attempts to exercise all the features of the MCP protocol. It is not intended to be a useful server, but rather a test server for builders of MCP clients, implementing prompts, tools, resources, sampling, and more.

All of these packages are available on npm under the @modelcontextprotocol organization scope.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps package and the GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY environment variable:

## Configuration

To use the Google Maps MCP server with npx, you need to configure it with the GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY environment variable in your MCP settings, using a command like: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps`

## Setup Instructions

The typical configuration in an MCP settings file should look like this:

The server is configured with stdio mode, using npx with the `-y` flag to run @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps, and the GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY environment variable must be set with your actual API key

## Getting Your API Key

To obtain your Google Maps API key, visit https://console.cloud.google.com/google/maps-apis/credentials and get your API key from your dashboard

## Known Issue

Users have reported getting "GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY environment variable is not set" errors even when the API key is provided in configuration files like cline_mcp_settings.json, which may require ensuring the environment variable is properly passed to the npx process.

Based on the search results, here's information about the `@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer` npx command and its environment variables configuration:

## Basic NPX Command

The basic npx command is: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer`

## Environment Variables Configuration

The server supports two key environment variables that can be configured in your MCP configuration file:

### 1. **PUPPETEER_LAUNCH_OPTIONS**

Set `PUPPETEER_LAUNCH_OPTIONS` with a JSON-encoded string in the MCP configuration's env parameter. This allows you to customize browser behavior with options like:
- `headless`: Whether to run in headless mode
- `executablePath`: Path to a specific Chrome/Chromium installation
- `args`: Additional browser arguments

### 2. **ALLOW_DANGEROUS**

This environment variable enables dangerous browser arguments (default: false). When set to `true`, it allows potentially risky launch options that could reduce security.

## Configuration Example

Here's an example configuration showing both environment variables:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-puppeteer": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer"],
      "env": {
        "PUPPETEER_LAUNCH_OPTIONS": "{ \"headless\": false, \"executablePath\": \"C:/Program Files/Google/Chrome/Application/chrome.exe\", \"args\": [] }",
        "ALLOW_DANGEROUS": "true"
      }
    }
  }
}
```


Note that these environment variables are passed through your MCP configuration file's `env` parameter, typically in your Claude Desktop or Claude Code CLI configuration.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about using the `@modelcontextprotocol/server-slack` package with the `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` and `SLACK_TEAM_ID` environment variables via npx:

## Configuration with npx

The package can be configured with `npx` using the command `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack`, passing environment variables `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` (Slack Bot Token starting with xoxb-) and `SLACK_TEAM_ID` (Slack Team ID starting with T).

## MCP Configuration Examples

Here are common ways to set this up:

**For AI clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor):**
The configuration uses `"command": "npx"`, `"args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-slack"]`, and environment variables `"SLACK_BOT_TOKEN": "xoxb-your-bot-token"` and `"SLACK_TEAM_ID": "T01234567"`.

**Docker Deployment:**
Docker configuration includes the same environment variables passed to the `mcp/slack` image.

## Important Notes

Granular tokens are now limited to 90 days and require 2FA by default, so you'll need to keep your bot token updated.

Note that the package is marked as "no longer supported," with a recommendation to contact Support at npm for more information.

content.text:
Based on my research, here are the correct npm package names and npx commands for the MCP servers you requested:

## 1. GitHub MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github`
- **Environment Variables**: `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` (your GitHub personal access token)
- **Note**: Development has moved to github/github-mcp-server repository

## 2. Puppeteer MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer`
- **Environment Variables** (optional):
  - `PUPPETEER_LAUNCH_OPTIONS` - JSON-encoded string for browser options (e.g., `{"headless": true}`)
  - `ALLOW_DANGEROUS` - Set to "true" to enable dangerous browser arguments (default: false)

## 3. Slack MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-slack`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack`
- **Environment Variables**: 
  - `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` (starts with xoxb-)
  - `SLACK_TEAM_ID` (starts with T)
- **Note**: This package is marked as "no longer supported" on npm

## 4. Filesystem MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /path/to/allowed/dir`
- **Environment Variables**: None required (path is passed as argument)
- **Note**: 171,238 weekly downloads, latest version 2026.1.14

## 5. Memory MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory`
- **Environment Variables**: None required
- **Note**: Knowledge graph-based persistent memory system, published 2026.1.26

## 6. Sequential Thinking MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking`
- **Environment Variables**: None required
- **Note**: Published 2025.12.18, structures problem-solving as explicit thought sequences

## 7. Brave Search MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search`
- **Environment Variables**: `BRAVE_API_KEY` (get from brave.com/search/api, free tier offers 2,000 queries/month)

## 8. Google Maps MCP Server
- **Package**: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps`
- **Command**: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps`
- **Environment Variables**: `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` (get from https://console.cloud.google.com/google/maps-apis/credentials)

All packages are licensed under the MIT License and published under the `@modelcontextprotocol` organization scope on npm.

## Sources:
- [GitHub MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-github)
- [Puppeteer MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer)
- [Slack MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-slack)
- [Filesystem MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem)
- [Memory MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory)
- [Sequential Thinking MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking)
- [Brave Search MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search)
- [Google Maps MCP Server - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@modelcontextprotocol/server-google-maps)
- [Model Context Protocol Servers GitHub](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers)
- [How to add Brave Search to Claude Desktop with MCP](https://brave.com/search/api/guides/use-with-claude-desktop-with-mcp/)
- [Example Servers - Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/examples)
